title: secale segetale subsp. dighoricum
intro: Secale segetale subsp. dighoricum, also known as wild spelt, is a type of wild grass belonging to the wheat family (Poaceae) and is closely related to wheat, rye, and barley. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Secale segetale subsp. dighoricum include its ability to aid in digestion, reduce inflammation, and support the immune system. Herbal preparations of wild spelt often involve infusions, decoctions, and teas, which can be used to alleviate various health issues, such as digestive problems and infections.
